name: langchain-subprocess-runnable
body:
LangChain pattern: wrap any stdin→stdout subprocess as a RunnableLambda.
Serialize input dict → JSON → stdin; run subprocess; parse stdout JSON → return dict.
Gives the process .invoke(), .batch(), .stream(), and | pipe support for free.
Used in C6 HookRunnable to e2e-test the UPS hook subprocess the same way Claude Code fires it.
Rule: if a program speaks JSON on stdin/stdout, it can be a typed Runnable — same interface as ChatAnthropic.

**Correction (2026-07-02):** the pipeline this tested was originally `memory_loader_lc.py` — that file no longer exists as source (only a stale compiled .pyc remains in claude-hooks/hooks/__pycache__/). It was refactored into `langchain_learning/session_graph.py` (build_session_graph(), StateGraph(SessionState)), driven via `hooks/dispatcher.py`'s `_handle_user_prompt_submit()` → `run_session()`. Same in dev/test/prod worktrees. If citing a file for the UPS pipeline, use session_graph.py, not memory_loader_lc.py.
